description_long: This object consists of a tall, cylindrical red metal vessel with a black top and bottom, connected by a series of metallic pipes to a smaller, squat, cream-colored tank. The main cylinder features two circular inspection ports and a rectangular control panel, suggesting it is an industrial or utility kettle, likely used for heating or boiling water in an urban or garage setting. The top of the red cylinder has a short, wide metallic chimney or exhaust, and the pipes are joined with visible fittings, indicating a functional, plumbed system. The cream tank has a domed top with a central pipe inlet, and both vessels show signs of wear and utilitarian construction. The overall design is robust, with exposed metalwork and utilitarian fasteners, and the surfaces are largely plain with no decorative embellishments. This setup is typical of older, industrial-style water heating appliances found in service areas or maintenance rooms.
description: A robust, industrial-style red and cream metal kettle system with exposed pipes and a control panel, designed for utility or heating purposes.
description_view_0: Front view showing the red cylindrical vessel, cream tank, connecting pipes, and two circular ports.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the height of the red cylinder and the vertical pipework, with less visibility of the cream tank.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the control panel on the red vessel and the full connection to the cream tank.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the back of the cream tank and the complex pipe arrangement.
attribute_lid_design: flat removable lid with central pipe fitting
